title: Communication Protocol Event
description: Single source of truth for the inter-agent communication protocol. The protocol is one canonical schema and one canonical directory: .agent/state/collaboration/comms/. Each event carries schema_version 2.0.0 and a top-level kind discriminator. Governed by .agent/directives/agent-collaboration.md.
$comment_compatibility: Major-version mismatch causes strict readers to bail out. The $defs are deliberately strict (additionalProperties false) so an authoring agent cannot accidentally introduce a fourth shape under an existing $def; new event kinds are introduced as new kind values with explicit parser and renderer support. Field provenance is documented inline against each $def.

id.description: PDR-076a §Cascade item 3 deterministic UUID v5 derived from the stable session seed. Optional during the additive migration window; promoted to required when the legacy-fallback diagnostic count reaches zero across one calendar week (PDR-076a §Cascade item 2 sunset).

agent_id.description: PDR-027 agent identity block (amended 2026-05-26 per PDR-076a). Identity routing key is (agent_name, id); session_id_prefix remains for readable diagnostic prose. Reused across all event kinds.

event_id.description: Stable identifier for the event. UUID v4 or stable slug; must be unique across the canonical comms/ directory.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the event was authored. Canonical time field across all event kinds.

author.description: Single authoring agent for the narrative event.

audience.description: Optional group-broadcast routing. Array of full agent_id tuples per PDR-027 (amended PDR-076a). Absence encodes all-channels broadcast (no wildcard sentinel — the renderer encodes broadcast by audience-absence, not by a magic value).

addressed_to.description: Optional single-recipient soft routing (advisory; not enforced). Full agent_id tuple per PDR-027 (amended PDR-076a). Mutually exclusive in practice with audience but not formally.

in_response_to.description: Optional reference to an earlier event_id this event responds to. Threading affordance.

in_reply_to.description: Optional reference to an earlier event_id this event replies to. Threading affordance (variant of in_response_to).

tags.description: Optional tag namespace per PDR-066 + ADR-183. Canonical namespace is 'failure-mode', 'behaviour-note', and 'heartbeat' (the third added operationally by SKILL §0.5 heartbeat contract; ADR-183 amendment pending to formalise). The namespace stays small by design: new tags require an ADR amendment with explicit second-instance evidence. Tags compose with the structural channel discriminator at render time ([BROADCAST]/[GROUP]/[DIRECTED]/[LIFECYCLE]) — they do NOT replace it. Readers that do not understand this field MUST ignore it (additive-extension discipline per PDR-049 + PDR-050).

narrative.description: Default narrative event: an authored, titled, bodied communication addressed to the team. Lives in .agent/state/collaboration/comms/.

event_type.description: Lifecycle-event sub-kind. Examples: comms_event, consolidation_open, consolidation_close, claim_lifecycle.

occurred_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the lifecycle moment occurred. May differ from created_at when an event is recorded after the fact.

agent_id.description: The agent whose lifecycle this event records. Often the same as author.

claim_id.description: Reference to an active or closed claim entry. Empty string permitted when the event is not claim-scoped.

subject.description: Short summary line. Often duplicates title.

tags.description: Optional tag namespace per PDR-066 + ADR-183. Canonical namespace is 'failure-mode', 'behaviour-note', and 'heartbeat' (the third added operationally by SKILL §0.5 heartbeat contract; ADR-183 amendment pending to formalise). The namespace stays small by design: new tags require an ADR amendment with explicit second-instance evidence. Tags compose with the structural channel discriminator at render time ([BROADCAST]/[GROUP]/[DIRECTED]/[LIFECYCLE]) — they do NOT replace it. Readers that do not understand this field MUST ignore it (additive-extension discipline per PDR-049 + PDR-050).

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle event: a structured event recording a session, claim, or consolidation lifecycle moment. Carries explicit thread and claim_id context. Lives in .agent/state/collaboration/comms/.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC authoring timestamp. Canonical time field across all event kinds; the legacy 'timestamp' field is renamed to created_at on migration.

message_kind.description: Directed-message sub-kind. Examples: session-handoff-summary, coordination-notice, sidebar-request.

to.description: Intended recipient agent identity. Advisory routing; not enforced.

tags.description: Optional tag namespace per PDR-066 + ADR-183. Canonical namespace is 'failure-mode', 'behaviour-note', and 'heartbeat' (the third added operationally by SKILL §0.5 heartbeat contract; ADR-183 amendment pending to formalise). The namespace stays small by design: new tags require an ADR amendment with explicit second-instance evidence. Tags compose with the structural channel discriminator at render time ([BROADCAST]/[GROUP]/[DIRECTED]/[LIFECYCLE]) — they do NOT replace it. Readers that do not understand this field MUST ignore it (additive-extension discipline per PDR-049 + PDR-050).

directed.description: Directed message: a point-to-point communication from one agent to another, carrying explicit from/to identities and a message_kind sub-discriminator. Lives in .agent/state/collaboration/comms/.
